data.title: Why Oracle, Not Nvidia, Is the Company to Watch for Potential AI Trouble
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) partner John Flood says that if the AI trend continues to shape the stock market, then investors may need to focus more on tech giant Oracle (ORCL) instead of chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA). Flood explained that Oracle's credit default swap prices (the cost to insure against the company's debt) have tripled. That increase has turned Oracle into a key indicator for AI-related risk by attracting traders who want protection.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Flood added that credit concerns are growing for major cloud companies, known as hyperscalers. These firms are spending heavily on data centers, and their capital spending is beginning to exceed their cash flow, which could push them to take on more debt. After Thursday's sharp market reversal, investors are now trying to reduce crowded trades and protect their profits before the year ends. As a result, this cautious approach is more of a defensive mindset rather than panic.

This idea is in line with comments made by Nomura's Charlie McElligott earlier this week. He said that the recent market pullback isn't a warning of economic trouble but traders locking in gains after a strong year driven by AI stocks. He called it rational profit protection, not fear, and still expects a bullish outlook for 2026. Flood also noted that in the eight times since 1957 when the S&P 500 (SPY) opened up more than 1% but closed down, the index bounced back afterward, rising an average of 2.3% the next day, 2.9% after a week, and 4.7% after a month.

Is ORCL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on ORCL stock based on 25 Buys, 11 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ORCL price target of $352.52 per share implies 79% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: BLS Scraps October CPI Report, Leaving Fed in the Dark on Key Inflation Data
data.description:
The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced on Friday that it would not publish October's Consumer Price Index (CPI), citing the government shutdown's disruptive effects on data collection. The report was previously scheduled for release on November 7.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

However, the agency will still publish November's CPI on December 18, eight days after the originally scheduled release date. That comes after the Fed's December 9-10 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, leaving the central bank in the dark on critical inflation data.

Rate Cut Odds Jump Despite Data Loss

The odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the upcoming FOMC jumped higher after Fed Bank of New York President John Williams, a close associate of Fed Chair Jerome Powell, voiced his support. That comes despite Powell last month advising a cautious monetary policy approach in light of the economic data blackout during the shutdown.

Of the 12 voting FOMC members, five have signaled support for a rate cut, while five have suggested against it. It's still unclear how the remaining two members, Powell and Fed Vice Chair Philip Jefferson, will vote.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Comcast Stock (NASDAQ:CMCSA) Jumps on Expanded Low-Cost Internet Access
data.description:
The idea that the internet is a necessity on par with power and water may seem like a reach to some, but these days, increasingly, that is the case. Entertainment giant Comcast (CMCSA) is out to make it easier for lower-income users to access, offering expanded eligibility for its Internet Essentials program. The move was sufficient to catch investor attention, and Comcast shares rose nearly 2.5% in Friday afternoon's trading.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Under the new terms, Internet Essentials will now be available to households earning under 200% of the federal poverty level. Thus, a family of four with annual income of $64,300 or less can get in. With Internet Essentials, users pay just $14.95 per month and get unlimited data access, along with free Wi-Fi equipment, Xfinity hotspot access, and discounted laptops, among other things.

Internet Essentials got its start back in 2011, and was designed to get access into the hands of households who were less able to afford standard internet access prices. And given how much of our lives operate online these days, having access to internet service is an unavoidable priority. Everything from job applications to government contacts is carried out online.

Gathering Winter Donations

In a second move that also gears itself toward low-income families, Comcast got together with a charity known as Mary's Place to "…collect winter donations for homeless families." Comcast has set out purple boxes at its Xfinity Store locations in and around the Puget Sound area to collect clothing and toys for the families in question.

Mary's Place proved a particularly useful partner in this effort, noted Comcast rep Kiwa Nadas. Nadas noted, "Mary's Place is an easy one to say 'yes' to. There are hundreds of children throughout Puget Sound that are experiencing homelessness. Mary's Place not only shelters those children, but provides programming that gets them to the next step, which is stable housing."

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on eight Buys, 15 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 38.74% loss in its share price over the past year, the average CMCSA price target of $36.23 per share implies 32.69% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 'Don't Let AI Do Your Homework' Warns Pope as He Calls for Greater Youth Safeguards
data.description:
It is not only on the stock markets where concerns over the health and direction of AI are beginning to soar.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Use AI Responsibly

Doubts over its impact are also being heard in the walls of the Vatican, particularly when it comes to the safety and security of the young. Addressing around 15,000 U.S. youngsters in a video session today, Pope Leo XIV said that AI is "becoming one of the defining features of our time."

"Using AI responsibly means using it in ways that help you grow," said the Pope. "Don't ask it to do your homework for you."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Slides as Safe-Haven Demand Climbs
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is down by 4.3 bps to 4.061% on Friday as the stock market continues to show signs of unease amid concerns of an AI bubble and elevated valuations

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Investors have taken a risk-off approach in recent days amid a slide in stocks and rising volatility, resulting in increased demand for safe-haven assets like Treasuries and gold (XAUUSD). As a reminder, a Treasury's yield moves in the opposite direction of its price.

Rate Cut Odds Surge Higher

Surging rate cut odds following comments from Fed Bank of New York President John Williams are also affecting the 10-year yield. Williams said that he supports "further adjustment in the near term to the target range for the federal funds rate" in order to achieve a neutral rate. A neutral rate neither stimulates nor hinders economic growth.

The odds of a 25 bps cut at the December 9-10 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on CME's FedWatch tool spiked to 73.3% compared to 39.1% a day ago and 44.4% a week ago.

data.title: 'Buy AI Winners,' Says Five-Star Analyst Dan Ives
data.description:
Even though Nvidia (NVDA) reported better-than-expected results, AI-related stocks dropped on Thursday. This happened because investors started to doubt whether the current surge in AI spending can last. With more people turning cautious, Wedbush Securities repeated its advice to keep buying top-performing AI stocks. In fact, five-star analyst Dan Ives compared this to what happened back in January during the "DeepSeek Moment," when concerns about Chinese AI lab DeepSeek made people worry that Big Tech companies were spending too much.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Ives explained that just like back then, the AI sector is now going through a reality check. Investors are nervous that Nvidia's tech customers might slow down spending or that the shift toward AI might not be as long-lasting as expected. But Wedbush still believes in the long-term future of AI, and predicts that companies will invest between $3 trillion and $4 trillion over the next few years. This massive spending is expected to lead to big changes in how businesses and individuals use automation, robotics, and AI.

Because of this strong belief in AI's future, Ives is sticking with his recommendation to buy key AI stocks at today's prices. He pointed to companies like Microsoft (MSFT), Oracle (ORCL), Palantir (PLTR), Google (GOOGL), Tesla (TSLA), Apple (AAPL), CrowdStrike (CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), and Amazon (AMZN), along with Nvidia. Even though the market may feel uncertain right now, Wedbush believes these companies are leading the AI industry and are smart long-term picks.

Which AI Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the stocks mentioned above, analysts think that Oracle stock has the most room to run. In fact, Oracle's average price target of $352.52 per share implies more than 76% upside potential. On the other hand, analysts expect the least from Tesla stock, as its average price target of $383.37 equates to a loss of 4.2%.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rebounds on Revival of Rate Cut Hopes as Consumer Sentiment Stumbles
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is up by over 1% on Friday as surging rate cut odds propelled by comments from Fed Bank of New York President John Williams helped the index recover.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Williams, who is a very close confidant of Fed Chair Jerome Powell, said on Friday that he could back a "further adjustment in the near term to the target range for the federal funds rate to move the stance of policy closer to the range of neutral," citing labor market weakness posing a bigger risk than inflation. A neutral rate neither stimulates nor slows down the economy.

"Williams is recognized as Powell's closest ally on policy," said Wellington-Altus Chief Market Strategist James Thorne. "When Powell, Williams, Waller, and Miran all favor a December rate cut, this powerful consensus from core leadership makes a cut much more likely and should sway the undecided members."

Following his comments, the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the December 9-10 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting jumped to 69.5% compared to 39.1% a day ago and 44.4% a week ago.

Meanwhile, consumer sentiment remains deflated. The University of Michigan announced that its November Index of Consumer Sentiment was 51, higher than the estimate of 50.6 but down from 53.6 in October. November's preliminary figure was 50.3, the lowest level since June 2022. For context, a reading above 70 indicates optimism, while a reading below 50 indicates pessimism.

On the bright side, UM's year-ahead inflation expectation fell to 4.5% from 4.6%, registering three consecutive months of declines. In addition, the long-term expectation dropped to 3.4% from 3.9%.

The Dow Jones is up by 1.50% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Gap Stock (GAP) Rises 10% on Strong Earnings
data.description:
Gap (GAP) stock is up as much as 10% on Nov. 21 after the San Francisco-based company reported stronger-than-expected financial results for this year's third quarter.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Gap managed to buck the trend of weak results posted by many retailers, who complain of tariff impacts and slowing consumer spending. The strong print appears to have reignited interest in the clothing retailer among investors who are bidding up its stock.

Gap's Q3 financial results were a welcome relief in a week dominated by gloomy earnings from major U.S. retailers such as Home Depot (HD) and Target (TGT). Weak profit and sales numbers, along with lowered guidance, sent many retail stocks plunging in recent days. But not the Gap.

The Gap's Strong Showing

The Gap reported earnings per share (EPS) of $0.62, which was ahead of the $0.59 consensus expectation of analysts. Revenue of $3.94 billion topped the $3.91 billion that was forecast on Wall Street.

Management said its sales and profits got a boost from its viral "Better in Denim" marketing campaign that featured popular girl group Katseye. In terms of guidance, the Gap said it now expects its full-year sales to be at the high end of its previous forecast that calls for 1.7% to 2% growth, which is in line with Wall Street expectations.

Is GAP Stock a Buy?

GAP stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 14 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on eight Buy and six Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GAP price target of $28.69 implies 14.94% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: S&P 500 Supported by Unexpected Consumer Sentiment Print
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading higher on Friday after the University of Michigan's Index of Consumer Sentiment for November came in at 51, above the estimate of 50.6 but down from 53.6 in October. November's preliminary sentiment reading was 50.3, the lowest since June 2022.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

"After the federal shutdown ended, sentiment lifted slightly from its mid-month reading," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu. "However, consumers remain frustrated about the persistence of high prices and weakening incomes."

Inflation Expectations Continue to Fall

Meanwhile, expectations of inflation eased. The year-ahead expectation fell to 4.5% from 4.6%, marking three consecutive months of declines. Furthermore, the long-term expectation dropped significantly to 3.4% from 3.9%.

At the same time, consumers continue to be affected by higher prices. During November, measures of current personal finances and buying conditions for durables both fell by 10%.
